characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: The titular main character of the show. He is some sort of freak (according to anyone he meets) whose parents were killed in a house fire and was taught by a shaman going by the name of Chief Master Guru. With his spiritual knowledge in adulthood, he mainly spends his time wandering from town to town to help those in need... only to fail miserably at it. AcCENT upon the Wrong SylLABle: A frequent feature of Xavier's bizarre speech patterns.Xavier:I cuntbea hypoChrist? I'llcommitsacricide. Ambiguously Evil: The show often flip-flops whether or not Xavier is intentionally harming people under the guise of saving them and to also stroke his own ego as well, or if he’s genuinely trying to be heroic, but due to hisBlue-and-Orange Morality, he ends up causing more issues by accident. It doesn’t help that in some instances where his help leads to worse things, he reacts with concern, but in other instances, he seems unfazed by it and thinks his assistance worked out. Blue-and-Orange Morality: Xavier seems to operate on a different value system than normal humans. His solutions to solving problems come from completely alien logic and he seems to be honestly unable to understand why his actions make people upset. Catchphrase: "Frittata!","Oooooh! Peep show!", and"Nooooooooo!!!"

characters.name: Xavier
characters.description: The titular main character of the show. He is some sort of freak (according to anyone he meets) whose parents were killed in a house fire and was taught by a shaman going by the name of Chief Master Guru. With his spiritual knowledge in adulthood, he mainly spends his time wandering from town to town to help those in need... only to fail miserably at it. AcCENT upon the Wrong SylLABle: A frequent feature of Xavier's bizarre speech patterns.Xavier:I cuntbea hypoChrist? I'llcommitsacricide. Ambiguously Evil: The show often flip-flops whether or not Xavier is intentionally harming people under the guise of saving them and to also stroke his own ego as well, or if he’s genuinely trying to be heroic, but due to hisBlue-and-Orange Morality, he ends up causing more issues by accident. It doesn’t help that in some instances where his help leads to worse things, he reacts with concern, but in other instances, he seems unfazed by it and thinks his assistance worked out. Blue-and-Orange Morality: Xavier seems to operate on a different value system than normal humans. His solutions to solving problems come from completely alien logic and he seems to be honestly unable to understand why his actions make people upset. Catchphrase: "Frittata!","Oooooh! Peep show!", and"Nooooooooo!!!"

characters.name: Xavier's Parents
characters.description: The parents of the main character, who weren't really the best towards him. Both are presumed dead before the show started, that is until Xavier's mother is revealed to be alive in the second season. Abusive Parents: They are both absolutely horrendous parents to Xavier, expressing nothing but contempt for him for seemingly any reason other than him being a "freak". May be subverted in the case of Xavier's father, who is only shown to be openly malicious to him in a potentially false memory - his ghost from Season 1 finale, while annoyed by his stupidity, doesn't seem to have any actual hatred for him. Asshole Victim:When Xavier's father is burned alive from the house fire, it's rather hard to feel sorry for him due to what a horrendous parent he was. Though it could likely be subverted considering the fact that he's never shown to be antagonistic towards Xavier save for a false memory he had.His mother also ends up in aBedlam Houseand gets lobotomized, and for the same reasons, it's hard to feel sorry for her considering her jerkassery. When Xavier's father is burned alive from the house fire, it's rather hard to feel sorry for him due to what a horrendous parent he was. Though it could likely be subverted considering the fact that he's never shown to be antagonistic towards Xavier save for a false memory he had. His mother also ends up in aBedlam Houseand gets lobotomized, and for the same reasons, it's hard to feel sorry for her considering her jerkassery.

characters.name: Computer
characters.description: A live action human operating a computer who provides information to Xavier whenever he needs help with a problem. Ambiguously Human: He appears to be an ordinary guy operating... well, a computer, but his weird way of talking, robotic-sounding voice, and the fact he always appears in a trippy background implies him to be an A.I. of sorts. Bumbling Sidekick: Although he only had two appearances in the show's run, he plays this role towards the just as dimwitted Xavier, assisting him by offering not-so-useful advice whenever he calls for him. Chuck Cunningham Syndrome: He suddenly stops showing up after his last appearance and he isn't called in by Xavier ever since. The Ditz: Rarely has any useful advice - he either states the obvious or at best discovers something way too late.

characters.name: Chief Master Guru
characters.description: Xavier's spiritual teacher after the death of his parents, however, his advice is less than helpful and most of the time he hurts Xavier. Ambiguous Situation: Despite being implied to be a fraud, he's also shown utilizing a variety of genuine mystical powers (such as him transforming into a buffalo, punching Xavier with his mind, and utilizing butterflies to cause a variety of effects), making it unclear how genuine he is about his mysticism. Exact Words: He shows Xavier that "the pain is all in your head" by striking him in the head in the shovel. Magical Native American: Parodied. He presents himself as a wise Native American Shaman with a variety of mystical powers, while also having genuine Indian traits (A Bindi, a Vishnu Statue on top of his Totem Pole, Devanagari ligatures and a Lotus Blossom on his Tepee and Xavier calls him a "Apachestani Shaman") However, it's also implied that he's a fraud and likely neither Indian or Native American. Mentor Occupational Hazard: Invoked. He attempted to fake his death in order to get rid of Xavier. It didn't seem to last long, though.

characters.name: Snake Hand
characters.description: A Day in the Limelight: He has a bigger role in "The 6th Teat of Good Intentions" and "El Tornadador", where he contributes to the plot by eating babies in the former episode and allowing Xavier to switch inside out and back to change his identity in the latter episode. Ambiguously Bi:Played for Laughs. In the third episode of Season 2, the snake hand zips over to a snake charmer and gives him a blowjob, Xavier quickly yanks him away and says "Snake Hand! I didn't know you was bi!" Depraved Bisexual: Played With in that he doesn't do anything depraved when it's revealed he's bi, him previouslyeating babieswould implicate him as this. Evil Hand: He acts as this for Xavier in one episode, eating the babies Xavier is taking care of despite Xavier's open resistance to it. Eats Babies: When Xavier unintentionally kidnaps the seven babies of a woman who were left unattended in a park to take them under his care, conflict arises from Xavier's snake hand's habit of eating the babies, eventually devouring all of them.

characters.name: Burbary Computer
characters.description: A.I. Is a Crapshoot: When Xavier asks it "What Doth Life?", it causes it to have a "depth overload", which causes all of the computers in the town to malfunction. When the computers are all dumped into the lake, it ends up spreading to the entire town and causes everyone to glitch out. Big Bad: It is the main cause of the first episode's conflict. Reality Warper: When all of the computers affected by its overload are dumped into a lake, it causes the town to slowly glitch out more and more.

characters.name: Locos
characters.description: Affably Evil: Despite being a cartel gang, they’re rather friendly to new members. The "evil" part gets dropped when Xavier makes them turn a new leaf. The Cartel: They're a Mexican gang who engage in drug trafficking and murder. Heel–Face Turn: After a couple of changes and suggestions from Xavier, they eventually stop their lives of crime and change for the better. Poke the Poodle: Aside from their typical violent crimes, they also force new recruits to watch them eat a pizza without getting a slice for themselves. Villainous Friendship: Although they're violent thugs, they also seem to genuinely care about each other as friends and have a shared love of grooming and training poodles. Parodied by a statement by one member of the gang.Loco:You guys are the only family I have. Maybe that's because you guys killed my family.

characters.name: Percy
characters.description: Queer Establishing Moment:Played for Laughs. He seems to have a knack for disabled men, given he immediately asked if his girlfriend was one when Xavier (who posed as him to protect him from the Locos) told him he has one. What Happened to the Mouse?: Subverted. After he's told by Xavier to stay in a sewer so the latter can cover up for him, before heaccidentally swallows toilet water mixed with cocaine, he's never mentioned again. Then he appears at the end where the mosquito figure declares him his love.

characters.name: Mosquito Figure
characters.description: Chekhov's Gun: After Xavierhelps a mosquito that pays him back by beating up two thugs, it lays eggs into his brain, which isn't brought up until he gets beaten up by Shiny,which hatch into numerous mosquitoes that form a humanoid shape and help defeat him. Curb-Stomp Battle: Incapacitates Shiny from a roundhouse kick in the head before throwing him into a vat of molten metal. The Worm That Walks: A humanoid figure made of millions of mosquitoes.

characters.name: Robbie
characters.description: A God Am I: A surprisingly subdued version. When he confronts his father-turned-Eversplosion, he tells him that he believes in God because he considershimselfGod due to creating life. Child Prodigy: He's a genius scientist who makes miraculous inventions despite still being a child. He even discovers how to create life. Genius Cripple: He's smart enough to make all sorts of revolutionary inventions and he uses a wheelchair. Hair of Gold, Heart of Gold: He's blonde and a generalNice Guy. Missing Mom: His mother died at some point before the events of his episode. Robbie is seen visiting her grave.

characters.name: Robbie's Dad/The Eversplosion
characters.description: Big Eater: If a bit of his dialogue is any implication, he tends to eat entire devil's foodcakes and once ate four of them in the span of one day. Driven to Suicide: After he finds out his faith-healing was a lie made by Robbie, he self-immolates in despair. However, it ends up making him become the Eversplosion. Fake Faith Healer: Double Subverted. He runs a Christian hospital which runs on faith healing. However, when we actually see an operation, it turns out that God himself appears to be performing surgeries and actually saving lives (through prayer, God performs a heart surgery by levitating a scalpel and other implements). But then it's revealed that Robbie is secretly manipulating the instruments using levers connected to strings and magnets, all without his father's knowledge. Fusion Dance: By self-immolating, he ends up fusing with the life Robbie created in a petri dish, becoming a living explosion known as the Eversplosion. Irony: Despite being a doctor, he thinks science is evil.

characters.name: The Everchild
characters.description: Anthropomorphic Personification: He claims to be the embodiment of everyone's inner child. Fusion Dance: The result of Xavier's snake hand eating 7 babies and then Xavier himself, effectively resulting in him being a fusion of Xavier and the various babies. Telepathy: Aside from being able to speak without a traditional mouth, he's also able to immediately understand the lives of others in his vicinity, including childhood memories. He ends updriving a cop to suicide because of it. Pun: His name, the Everchild, seems to be a pun about Xavier being a perpetual manchild.

characters.name: Chief Worthington
characters.description: Corrupt Corporate Executive: He starts off as an archetypical greedy oil baron, and when injected with Native American blood, he uses it as an excuse to open a casino to get even richer. Laser-Guided Karma: Chief Worthington claims thatby injecting himself with more Indian blood up the legal limit, he'll get richer. Thus, he and his crew dig for Indian blood at the Ancient Indian Burial Ground. The result: the blood transforms into an army ofIndian blood humanoids that attack Worthington's oil crewmen and raid his casino, wherethey kill anyone on sight. Native American Casino: After he gets injected with Indian blood, he uses this as an excuse to open a casino. Politically Incorrect Villain: Ooh boy, seeing the ad for hisNative American Casinoand seeing all of its audaciously offensive jokes will have calling him this to be an understatement.

characters.name: Worthington Jr.
characters.description: Break the Haughty: Xavier makes him experience suffering for the first time, causing him to realize pain is real and to wish to use his father's wealth to better the world. Rich in Dollars, Poor in Sense: Even after reforming, he thinks suffering and universal oneness are goods that can be purchased with money. Spoiled Brat: He starts the episode as a spoiled, bratty rich kid who's so privileged that he doesn't even know that suffering exists. However.... Took a Level in Kindness: He goes from a selfish brat to wishing to get his father to give up his greedy ways to improve the world after Xavier shows him the existence of suffering. Unexplained Accent: Despite his father having a Texan accent, he inexplicably has a British accent. It could be his mother is British.

characters.name: Nimgok
characters.description: All Cavemen Were Neanderthals: He fits this trope to a tee, being big and strong while also havingHulk Speakand a unibrow. Domestic Abuse: He's physically abusive to his wife. He claims he hits her because he loves her. He does seem to get better by the end of the episode, though. Dumb Muscle: He's strong, but not very smart. Obliviously Evil: Implied. Considering how his wife says that he hits her because he loves her, and he doesn’t seem to notice what he’s doing wrong, he’s presumably unaware that his actions are not a way to express love.

characters.name: Farmer
characters.description: Badass Normal: Despite being nothing but an ordinary farmer, he manages to fight a horde of corn mutants and stop an apocalypse largely by himself (although Xavier takes the credit). Blasphemous Boast: Before shooting God, he says this: Did You Just Punch Out Cthulhu?: He gets so frustrated that he actually causes God tobleed.Albeit with downsides. Enraged by Idiocy: He eventually grows so enraged by Xavier not remembering how to do a dance that he almost shoots him, but ends up shooting God instead. Gaia's Vengeance: He inadvertently causes this via shooting God, which makes aRain of Bloodthat causes all the nature in and around the city to become sentient and take its toll on humanity.

characters.name: Squatopia
characters.description: Anarchy Is Chaos: Averted. Although they're said to have no rules, Squatopia isn't shown to be particularly chaotic or lawless. Cloudcuckoolander: They're so weird that they're able to make Xavier himself look like theOnly Sane Manby comparison. Godwin's Law: They believe that any form of rules is fascism, so they punish anyone having rules by forcing them to wear the scarlet F. Go Out with a Smile: When they're burned to death after the explosion of the Burning Person structure, they don't seem upset. In fact, they're quite happy to be set on fire. Hypocritical Humor: They only have one rule: no rules. Anyone who has rules is punished by wearing the scarlet F (standing forFascist).

characters.name: Xavier's Sperm
characters.description: Animal Companion: He serves as this for Xavier and Tude after he's created when the former tries to get money via "seed", much to Xavier's chagrin. All Animals Are Dogs: He acts a lot like a dog, trying to play fetch with Xavier and digging. The Cameo: He makes a brief appearance in "Damnesia Vu" after Xavierpulls of his own sacrifice on the sun god an Aztec tribe makes sacrifices to. Cute Little Fangs: He has a noticable snaggletooth. Leitmotif: A whimsical-sounding tune plays whenever he interacts with one of the characters.

characters.name: Popo the Preaching Gorilla
characters.description: Bestiality Is Depraved: A priest was given the idea to have sex with her from a fortune teller. Although this is implied to have been a trick. Easy Evangelism: Somehow manages to makeeveryoneconvert to Christianity. Expy: Based on a real gorilla known for using sign language namedKoko. Knight of Cerebus: A non-antagonistic example. The episode she's focused on is probably the creepiest one in the series. There's still the usualSurreal Humor, but it noticeably leans the most toSurreal Horror. Mobile-Suit Human: More like Mobile Suit Animal, but the ending implies her to be this controlled by a chimpanzee.

characters.name: Seamus
characters.description: The Antichrist: Implied. The priest taking care of him says he was a Virgin birth, and along with his generally evil behavior, implicates him as the opposite of Jesus. Enfant Terrible: He's a toddler, and anevillittle bastard. Heel–Face Turn: He immediately stops being evil the moment he swallows a Fiddelin pill. Pedophile Priest: Inverted. Xavier thinks the Priest caring for him is molesting him, but as it turns outhe's the one molesting the Priest. Slasher Smile: Frequently pulls these off whenever he talks about what he did to his victims.

characters.name: Fiddelin Monks
characters.description: Artistic License – Medicine: Obviously, pills can't be made out of sounds like monk chants. Lotus Position: Are in this pose when they chant into their pipes to produce Fiddelin. Morton's Fork: Xavier yells at them for working, and six months later he still yells at them when they don't work and instead have the Vibulator 2000 chant for them. Nice Guy: Befitting their stereotypical monk behavior, they are fairly polite towards Xavier even though he's trying to make them stop working. They also calmly oblige to Xavier's protests when they eventually cave in from his mannerisms. Suddenly Ethnicity: Despite allegedly being Tibetan monks, when they stop working and play video games in the Fiddlein headquarters instead, they randomly develop Hispanic accents. Lampshaded by Xavier calling them "Chin-canos" after kicking the Vibulator 2000.

characters.name: Don Ho
characters.description: Devil in Plain Sight: He clearly behaves like stereotypical gangster andtalks like one, but Xavier realizes that only after he mentions that he is one. The Don: As his name indicates, he acts like your stereotypical mafia don. He actually mixes a bunch of different mob stereotypes. Eye Scream: One of many injuries he gets from the Ultimate Hitman ishis own toenailthrown into his eye. Heel–Face Door-Slam: After aHeel Realizationhe tries to change his ways, but this didn't stop him from being killed by his own hitman. Heel Realization: When Xavier shoves a briefcase full of After-Light into his face, he sees all the harm he's done and devotes his life to spreading joy.

characters.name: Disgrazio/Dale
characters.description: Camp Straight: He talks in a high pitched voice and loves singing and candy (at least as his cover identity) but doesn't display any overt signs of being gay. Clark Kenting: It's pretty clear how fake his disguise is, but despite that, he's managed to stay undercover for12 yearswithout Don Ho suspecting anything. The only reason he was figured out was because Xavier directly tells Don Ho. Didn't Think This Through: His whole disguise is a result of this. He says he just freeballed his character and that he made up the singing henchmen identity out of nervousness while the candy thing just "happened". Undercover Cop Reveal: He reveals to Xavier that he's actually an undercover cop, something which proves to be a big mistake when Don Ho set the Ultimate Hitman upon him.

characters.name: The Ultimate Hitman
characters.description: Cannibalism Superpower: Don Ho fed him starfish and other regenerative animals, and because of that, hegot their regeneration powers. The Dog Bites Back: The first thing he does after becomingGrey Goois kill Don Ho for his abusive treatment. Driven to Suicide: He doesnotlikebeing immortal, and tries to do himself in, only tomultiplyeach attempt. He's finally done in when he turns intoThe Monolith. Grey Goo: He becomes one aftertrying to kill himself. Healing Factor: Afterbeing fed with starfishhe himself became self regenerating. Only problem is thathe wants to kill himselfandthat power makes it impossible.

characters.name: Dark Notion/Baron Society Weinberg
characters.description: Body Horror: Under his coat, he has a ton of piercings and self-harm scars which arenotpretty on the eyes. Dark Is Not Evil: Although he has a disturbing knack for self-harm and is a gothic-dressing person who behaves a bit oddly, he is a relatively affable guy who genuinely sees Xavier as a friend and is willing to help his causes despite how outlandish they are. Though he did beat him up once when looking for his "slimy chum" (actually Xavierturned inside-out posing as someone else). "Darkness von Gothick" Name: He initially calls himself "Dark Notion", but switches to "Baron Society Weinberg" due to his belief that society is oppressive, then switches back to his previous alias when it doesn't work out in order to clear confusion between him and society itself. Goth: He certainly dresses Emo and expresses the belief that he'soppressed by society. Self-Harm: Exaggerated. Aside from the scars on his arms, his body is also covered in scaring under his coat. He even scratches his new alias on his torso and also scratches it out in order to clear confusion between him and society itself.

characters.name: Eric
characters.description: All There in the Manual: His name isn't stated in the episode once, however, the special guest credit mentions that his name is Eric. Asshole Victim: He is the first person to be shown sucked up by the giant tornado the smaller tornadoes merge into, but considering he also enslaved said tornadoes, this is pretty much his karma for doing so. Jerk with a Heart of Gold: Despite enslaving tornados to do cotton-picking and even making up a slur for them, he seems to honestly value his friendship with Xavier and even considers him a "triple-uber bromigo-sabe deluxe" for finding the people who freed his tornadoes (despite one of whom just being Xavier inside-out). Living Whirlwind: He creates several small living whirlwinds to act as his servants, ones that he ends up turning into his slaves. Odd Friendship: He forms one with Xavier, being a relatively normal weather technician who eventually uses science to enslave wind, while Xavier is a highly spiritualHumanoid AbominationandIdiot Hero.

characters.name: Hobo
characters.description: Crazy Homeless Person: A homeless man who treats adhesives (including staples and cement) as drugs and frequently has visions of a war he wasn’t even apart of. Fantastic Drug: He eventually starts huffing Aura after Xavier teaches him how to see it and describes it as the "ultimate ethereal glue". G-Rated Drug: Treats glue and other adhesives like drugs. The Stoner: Parodied. He acts like a stereotypical stoner, but instead of weed, he uses glue and a variety of other adhesives as drugs. He even references the war on drugs with weed being substituted with glue. Shell-Shocked Veteran: Subverted. He says he has nightmares of war, indicating that he's a veteran,but it turns out to be a lie: He tried to sign up for war but they told him he was "too crazy to kill."

characters.name: Big Papa Dick
characters.description: Fetishes Are Weird: He runs a strip club entirely built on pregnancy fetishism, something which is presumably based on his own desire to get pregnant. Motor Mouth: Downplayed. Whenever he's speaking on the microphone, he talks at a more faster, yet still comprehensible pace. Mr. Seahorse: This is his ultimate goal. He's always found the sight of a pregnant woman beautiful and wants to experience that joy for himself.

characters.name: Inkblot
characters.description: Ambiguously Evil: It isn't clear if he's meant to be seen as a villain or not given he's also supposed to represent Xavier's pent-up violence. The way he introduces himself once he starts to escape with Xavier's mother is presented the way a villain representing someone's inner demon would do. But aside frombrutally killinga doctor (who even then pretty much had it coming) and taking his mother, he never does anything else malicious,even finding Xavier's way of using reverse psychology by killing random people despite saying he doesn't pretty messed up. Not to mention his introductory scene has him trying to get Xavier to realize his perspective on things (though he fails due to Xavier being, well,Xavier). The Conscience: He claims to be a representation of Xavier's conscience, as well as his pent up violence too. Enraged by Idiocy: After a few seconds of listening to Xavier's inane comments, he gets pissed off and somehow ends up at the psychiatrist's office in place of Xavier to calm down. Unfortunately, the inkblot test he gets just happened to look like Xavier. Last Episode, New Character: He is one of the last secondary characters Xavier encounters in the series finale, "Braingea's Final Cranny". Only Sane Man: Despite being a living inkblot, he's arguably the closest thing the series has to an actually normal character, which doesn’t really bode well when you’re the conscience of a weirdHumanoid Abominationwith an extremely loose grip on reality and helping people.

characters.name: Asylum Doctor
characters.description: Asshole Victim: Gets brutally stabbed by the Inkblot. While his corpse is prettygruesome, given he had no qualms about trying to revitalizea controversial medical practice, nor tricking someone into having sex withtheir own mother, it's hard not to say he had it coming. For Science!: The reason he tricked Xavier into having sex with his mother was for a test (an institutionalized one, no less) regarding brain transplants and incest taboos. Last Episode, New Character: He is one of the last secondary characters Xavier encounters in the series finale, "Braingea's Final Cranny". Morally Ambiguous Doctorate: Runs an asylum that specializes inlobotomyand orders patients to be shocked with aShock Stick. He also tricks Xavier into having sex with his mother. There Is No Kill Like Overkill:While it's hard to say he didn't have some form of karma comingfor cruelly treating his patients,lobotomizingthem, and tricking Xavier into having sex with his mother, his corpse after he gets stabbed by the Inkblot (and that wasonestab mind you) is definitely not a pretty sight.
